description: The HSTU model introduces a non-softmax attention mechanism for sequential recommendation, using SiLU-gated attention with a 0.0 masking strategy to avoid softmax issues. It employs L2-normalized output embeddings and temperature-scaled logits for stable sampled softmax training. The model is applied to the Amazon Beauty dataset under a leave-one-out evaluation protocol, replacing the standard softmax-based SASRec approach.
motivation: To address the limitations of softmax attention in sequential recommendation, such as the fully-masked-row NaN issue and the need for stable training with sampled softmax, by leveraging a gated SiLU-based attention mechanism.
implementation_notes: The attention mechanism uses SiLU activation on attention scores and masks with 0.0 instead of -inf, preventing the NaN problem that occurs when all rows are masked in softmax. Output embeddings are L2-normalized, and logits are temperature-scaled for sampled softmax, improving training stability.
pseudocode: Not provided, but the core steps could be: 1) Compute attention scores; 2) Apply SiLU activation; 3) Mask with 0.0 for padding/ causal positions; 4) Compute weighted sum; 5) L2-normalize output embeddings; 6) Use temperature-scaled logits for sampled softmax loss.
